categories.name: Networking
categories.description: Managed networking services to connect your workloads securely

services.name: VPC
services.description: Isolated virtual networks with standard CIDR allocation
guardrails: CIDR ranges are allocated centrally to avoid conflicts
All traffic is logged and retained for 90 days

services.name: Transit Gateway
guardrails: Cross-account peering must go through Transit Gateway

services.name: CloudFront
services.description: CDN for static assets and API acceleration
guardrails: Direct internet ingress requires WAF attachment

categories.name: Compute
categories.description: Managed compute services to run your applications at scale

services.name: ECS (Fargate)
services.description: Serverless containers — no infrastructure to manage
guardrails: Containers run as non-root by default
All compute must reside in private subnets

services.name: EKS
guardrails: All compute must reside in private subnets

services.name: Lambda
guardrails: Lambda functions have a 15-minute timeout maximum

services.name: EC2
services.description: Virtual machines (for workloads that need them)
guardrails: EC2 instances must use approved AMIs
Instance types are limited to approved families (cost control)
All compute must reside in private subnets

categories.name: Storage & Data
categories.description: Managed storage and database services for your applications

services.name: S3
services.description: Object storage with encryption at rest
guardrails: S3 buckets are private by default — no public access

services.name: RDS
guardrails: RDS instances must be in private subnets
Database credentials managed via Secrets Manager

services.name: EFS
services.description: Shared file system for containers and EC2

categories.name: Security
categories.description: Security services to protect your workloads and meet compliance requirements

services.name: IAM
services.description: Role-based access control and service accounts
guardrails: IAM policies are reviewed and constrained by permission boundaries

services.name: WAF
services.description: Web application firewall for public-facing services
guardrails: WAF is mandatory for any internet-facing endpoint

services.name: Secrets Manager
services.description: Secure storage for credentials and API keys
guardrails: All secrets must be stored in Secrets Manager (no hardcoded credentials)
